findings.signature: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS skill_nodes/skill_edges/schema_version/skill_graph_metadata/vec_metadata + 6 indexes
findings.notes: Core DDL uses standard CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS, CHECK constraints, AUTOINCREMENT, FK with ON DELETE CASCADE — all supported by Turso except vec_metadata usage patterns downstream.

findings.signature: db.pragma('journal_mode = WAL') with isWalFallbackError guard falling back to DELETE
findings.notes: Turso/libSQL has limited pragma support; WAL mode is default in libSQL so the fallback to DELETE is unnecessary but the .pragma() call itself must be replaced with Turso config.

findings.signature: db.pragma('busy_timeout = 5000'), db.pragma('foreign_keys = ON'), db.pragma('wal_checkpoint(FULL)')
findings.notes: better-sqlite3 .pragma() API has no direct Turso equivalent; busy_timeout and foreign_keys must be set via PRAGMA statements or connection options; wal_checkpoint(FULL) may not be available.

findings.signature: ensureVecTableForDim(database, 768); ensureVecTableForDim(database, 1024) — creates vec_<dim> virtual tables via sqlite-vec
findings.notes: sqlite-vec virtual tables (vec0 module) require loadExtension() which Turso does not support; this blocks vector embedding storage entirely.

findings.signature: database.prepare('PRAGMA table_info(skill_nodes)').all() — column introspection for migration
findings.notes: PRAGMA table_info is supported in Turso but the .prepare() + .get() pattern must change to Turso async driver.

findings.signature: database.exec(SCHEMA_SQL) — executes multi-statement DDL string
findings.notes: better-sqlite3 .exec() is synchronous multi-statement; Turso JS driver uses async .execute() or .batch() for multi-statement execution.

findings.signature: const tx = database.transaction(() => { ... }); tx()
findings.notes: better-sqlite3 synchronous transaction API; Turso uses .batch() for transactional multi-statement execution — different API shape.

findings.signature: checkSqliteIntegrity(dbPath) called in initDb before open
findings.notes: Integrity probe runs quick_check via read-only better-sqlite3 handle; Turso embedded has different corruption semantics.

findings.signature: new Database(databasePath, { readonly: true, fileMustExist: true }) — separate read-only handle
findings.notes: Read-only handle pattern for shadow scoring; Turso embedded may not support fileMustExist option or separate readonly connections.

findings.signature: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema_version (version INTEGER NOT NULL) + version check/insert/update logic
findings.notes: Simple version tracking table — portable to Turso; no constructs blocking migration.

findings.signature: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS vec_metadata (key TEXT PRIMARY KEY, value TEXT NOT NULL, created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')))
findings.notes: Metadata table for embedder pointer state; schema is portable but ensureVecMetadataTable import may contain sqlite-vec coupling.

findings.signature: WeakMap<Database.Database, PreparedStatements> caching 12 prepared statements
findings.notes: better-sqlite3 synchronous Statement objects cached in WeakMap; Turso async driver uses different Statement type — cache pattern must adapt.

findings.signature: BFS loop with application-level visited set, MAX_TRANSITIVE_DEPTH=8, no WITH RECURSIVE
findings.notes: Avoids recursive CTEs — graph traversal is application-side BFS, which is Turso-compatible by design.

findings.signature: SELECT n.*, COUNT(e.id) AS inbound_count FROM ... GROUP BY n.id HAVING COUNT(e.id) >= ?
findings.notes: Standard GROUP BY/HAVING aggregation — portable to Turso.

findings.signature: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS skill_graph_daemon_lease (workspace_key TEXT PRIMARY KEY, owner_id TEXT, pid INTEGER, acquired_at INTEGER, heartbeat_at INTEGER)
findings.notes: Separate lease DB file with single-row PK table; DDL is Turso-compatible but uses PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL inline in .exec().

findings.signature: db.exec('PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL; CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ...') — pragma embedded in exec string
findings.notes: PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL inside .exec() multi-statement; Turso libSQL may reject PRAGMA in batch/execute or handle differently.

findings.signature: db.transaction(() => { SELECT ... WHERE workspace_key = ?; INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE ... })
findings.notes: Lease acquisition is a synchronous better-sqlite3 transaction; Turso async .batch() replaces this — critical for single-writer daemon model.

findings.signature: db.pragma('busy_timeout = 1000') on separate lease database
findings.notes: Lease DB has its own busy_timeout via .pragma() API; must migrate to Turso connection-level config.

findings.signature: new Database(dbPath) per attempt with SQLITE_BUSY retry loop (3 attempts)
findings.notes: Opens fresh better-sqlite3 handle per retry; Turso embedded connection pooling may change retry semantics.

findings.signature: new Database(dbPath, { readonly: true, fileMustExist: true }); db.prepare('PRAGMA quick_check').get()
findings.notes: PRAGMA quick_check on readonly handle; Turso embedded may not support quick_check or fileMustExist option.
